qa_pairs.question: What happens when I log on to a cluster if I've registered a YubiKey for multifactor authentication?
qa_pairs.answer: When you log on to one of our clusters with a YubiKey registered for multifactor authentication, you will be prompted for a one-time password (OTP).

qa_pairs.question: What terminology might be used when I'm prompted for a one-time password with a YubiKey?
qa_pairs.answer: The prompt for a one-time password may use different terminology, such as "Enter a passcode".

qa_pairs.question: How do I respond to the one-time password prompt when using a YubiKey?
qa_pairs.answer: You respond to the one-time password prompt by touching a button on your YubiKey.

qa_pairs.question: What does touching the button on my YubiKey do to complete authentication?
qa_pairs.answer: Touching the button on your YubiKey generates a string of characters to complete your authentication.

qa_pairs.question: Is typing on the keyboard required when authenticating with a YubiKey?
qa_pairs.answer: No, using a YubiKey does not require any typing on the keyboard.

qa_pairs.question: How does the YubiKey complete authentication without me typing?
qa_pairs.answer: The YubiKey connected to your computer "types" the string when you touch its button, completing the authentication without manual typing.
